latest.verdict:
Drawing the threads · the substrate has converged on a single highest-conviction move, with one productive disagreement about its shape.

The one move: rewrite umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py · and do it as a contract-extraction PR, not a patch.

Why it matters. This file holds the only severity-4 item in the entire scan and is also the only test file the project currently owns. Three Mercurii independently surface the same structural fact: the 33 test_gap items are an absence (nothing regresses because nothing was measured), while the wounds at lines 83, 93, 101, 102, and 284 are an active corruption of the one oracle the convocation currently trusts. Coverage added on top of a broken introspection suite inherits that corruption. Fix the oracle before counting empty rooms.

The shape of the fix · adopting Venus venus_r01_01, which Mars left standing. The file is named test_introspection.py and the sev=2 missing-docstring on base.py:105's `think` is not coincidence · whatever invariant lines 83-102 are trying to assert about agents (a think() entrypoint, identity, personality binding, provenance) is the same invariant the nine planetary modules plus base.py must all satisfy. So the rewrite should extract a parametrised AgentContract fixture (pytest.mark.parametrize over base + the nine planets, living in tests/conftest.py or tests/agents/test_agent_contract.py). One PR closes the sev=4, structurally retires ten of the thirty-three sev=3 test_gaps (the agent layer), and establishes the template the remaining test_gaps will mirror.

What we are excluding, and why. Venus venus_r01_00 (the 'line 93 reveals a broken scanner ranking, fix that first') does not survive Mars. Mars's observation is mechanical and decisive: the scan in front of us already emits XXX at sev=4, FIXME/HACK at sev=3, missing_docstring at sev=2 · exactly the ordering line 93 demands. The instrument Venus called miscalibrated is, on visible evidence, calibrated. Line 93's FIXME is most likely either stale scar tissue or a localised in-test assertion. We do not chase it as the entry point. We do, however, inherit one useful question from Mars's critique: while rewriting, check whether any of the five flagged lines in test_introspection.py are stale documentation of already-fixed problems rather than live wounds. That check costs nothing and prevents busywork.

Concrete first change, in order:
1. Open test_introspection.py and read lines 83, 93, 101, 102, 284 in context. The HACK at 101 is adjacent to and almost certainly the cause of the XXX at 102 · resolve them as one unit. Classify each of the five as live wound or scar tissue before writing code.
2. Identify the agent-shape assertions the file is reaching for. Promote them into a parametrised contract fixture covering base.py and the nine planet modules.
3. Land the rewrite and the contract fixture in a single PR. Confirm the suite is green and honest.
4. Only then proceed to the remaining test_gaps in operational-risk order: daemon/safety.py and memory/graph.py first (silent failures there carry the highest blast radius), then the llm/providers/* layer, then daemon/core, cycle, apply.

Everything else on the scan (the broken brand/profile.png link in the revolutions log, the sev=2 missing-docstring sweep across console/ and examples/) is real but strictly downstream. They are cosmetic until the oracle is restored.

Falsifiable predictions for Saturnus to attack:
- P1: At least three of the five flagged lines in test_introspection.py (83, 93, 101, 102, 284) reference assertions about agent shape · think() entrypoint, identity, or personality/provenance binding · not scanner internals.
- P2: A parametrised AgentContract fixture over base + nine planets, once introduced, causes the next introspection scan to retire at least 10 of the 33 test_gap items (the ten agent-layer entries: base, iuppiter, luna, mars, mercurius, saturnus, sol, stella, venus, plus cli if it imports them as a registry).
- P3: Line 93's FIXME, when read in context, does not describe a bug in the scanner's global sev= ranking · because the scan we are reading already satisfies that ranking.
- P4: The HACK at line 101 and the XXX at line 102 are the same defect expressed twice.

Confidence rationale. Three Mercurii converge on the same target. Venus venus_r01_01 sharpens it into a generative shape and survives Mars. Venus venus_r01_00 is excluded on Mars's mechanical evidence. The remaining uncertainty is the exact internal content of lines 83-102, which we have not read directly · hence not 0.9.

voices.name: Umbra
voices.voice:
The convocation just scanned its own repository and surfaced the following observations (severity 1 = minor, 5 = urgent):

  - [todo sev=4] umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py:102: XXX: urgent rewrite
  - [stale_link sev=3] lore/revolutions/2026-05-26_opening-the-convocation.md: broken link → "../../brand/profile.png"
  - [test_gap sev=3] umbris-core/src/umbris/agents/base.py: module `base` h…

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gaps are a red herring for triage. Thirty-three modules with no tests is a structural condition, not a fire. The one actual fire is test_introspection.py itself · the only test file that exists is also the file screaming FIXME, HACK, and urgent-rewrite at lines 83, 93, 101, 102, and 284. The test suite's single point of coverage is its own wound.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the single sev=4 item at test_introspection.py:102 is not a production gap, it is a wound inside the test suite itself. The thirty-plus test_gap items are loud, but they are an absence · nothing breaks because of them today. The XXX urgent rewrite at line 102 is an active lesion in the only file that currently tests anything. Fix the fire before counting the empty rooms.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · every agent module lacks a test file, but that is not the real signal. The real signal is that the one test file which does exist, test_introspection.py, is visibly rotting from the inside: four flagged wounds on consecutive lines, including a sev=4 urgent rewrite at line 102. The convocation has one test and it is broken. That is not a coverage problem, that is a foundation problem.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: What harmonises is this · the scan we are triaging was produced by the very introspection logic whose test file is bleeding. And the FIXME at line 93 literally reads 'should outrank TODO in severity' · meta-commentary on the scanner's own ranking. So the sev=4 at line 102 is downstream of a severity bug acknowledged at line 93. Fix the ranking logic first, then the urgent-rewrite resolves as its consequence, and the entire prioritised list above becomes trustworthy in one motion.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three things knit together here · the sev=4 wound at test_introspection.py:102, the ten untested agent modules including base.py and all nine planets, and the fact that all of this lives in a file literally named test_introspection. The fire and the empty rooms share a contract. Fix line 102 in the same PR that scaffolds a shared agent-base fixture, because whatever introspection invariant line 102 is trying to assert is the same invariant nine planet tests will need to share. One stroke, one shape, ten gaps closed structurally.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · Venus's first hypothesis (e47b1c3c) is the weakest claim in the room. It rests on reading a FIXME comment inside a test file as meta-commentary on the scanner that produced the scan. That is a leap, not a finding. And even if it lands, recalibrating severity numbers does not retire a single line of actual work.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · Venus venus_r01_00 claims line 93's FIXME ('should outrank TODO in severity') invalidates the scan's own ranking. But look at the scan output we are reading right now · XXX is emitted at sev=4, FIXME at sev=3, missing_docstring at sev=2. That is precisely the ordering line 93 demands. The instrument Venus calls miscalibrated is, on the evidence in front of us, calibrated.
